title: Marinated German Sausage Sandwich
description: A briny, pillowy sandwich that turns a lazy Sunday into a flavorful German feast, featuring Worcestershire sauce, pickles, and Mediterranean-friendly flavors.
instructions: In a shallow dish combine worcestershire sauce, lemon juice, olive oil, minced garlic, and smoked paprika; stir to create a marinade.
Add sausages to the marinade, ensuring each is coated; cover and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
While sausages marinate, slice rolls lengthwise and lightly toast them in a dry skillet over medium heat until golden.
Heat a grill or cast‑iron pan to 350°F; cook sausages for 5 minutes per side until browned and cooked through, internal temp 160°F.
Remove sausages from heat and let rest 2 minutes before slicing into rings.
Spread mustard on the toasted roll halves; layer salad greens, pickle slices, and sausage rings.
Drizzle a small amount of the leftover marinades over the assembled sandwich.
Close the sandwich, cut in half, and serve immediately.
